emails.body:
Megan —

Saw this listing pop up late last night. 312 Cedar Point, four bed, two and a half bath, quarter acre. Photos look great but you know how that goes. Asking $685k.

James is on shift until 1900 but I’d love to walk it this weekend if it’s still available. Can you pull the disclosures and see if there’s an offer deadline?

Also looping Daniel so he can run the numbers on our preapproval if we decide to move fast.

Thanks,
Emily

Emily —

Pulled it up. Listed Thursday, so it’s fresh. No offer deadline yet but the sellers are reviewing Sunday evening. Disclosures are clean — roof 2019, HVAC 2021, no water intrusion flags. HOA is $185/mo, covers common-area landscaping and the little pocket park at the end of the cul-de-sac.

I can get us in Saturday 10:00 or Sunday 14:00. Let me know what works and I’ll confirm with the listing agent.

Megan

Emily —

Per Megan’s note, I ran a quick scenario at $685k with 20% down. Principal & interest comes in around $3,580 at today’s 6.875%. Add taxes (~$7,200/yr), insurance (~$1,800/yr), and the HOA, you’re looking at roughly $4,450/mo all-in.

Your preapproval letter is good to $725k purchase price, so there’s room if it goes over asking. Happy to update the letter Saturday morning if you want to submit same day.

Attached a one-pager with the breakdown. Let me know if you want to tweak down payment or rate assumptions.

— Dan

Em —

Saw the thread. Numbers look doable on paper but I want to stress-test a few things before we commit:

1. Childcare runs $1,600/mo for both kids starting January. That’s not in the monthly nut above.
2. My orders could cut BAH if we PCS in 2027. Need to model rent-out vs. sell scenarios.
3. Emergency fund drops below three months if we drain savings for the 20% down + closing.

Can we sit down with the spreadsheet tonight? I’ll bring the laptop home.

— James

Megan —

James raised solid points. Childcare and the BAH uncertainty are real. I’d still like to see the house Saturday — looking doesn’t cost us anything — but we may need to adjust our max offer down to $660k to keep the cushion.

Can you still get us the 10:00 slot? And if the sellers counter above $660k, I’d rather walk than stretch.

Emily

Emily —

10:00 Saturday confirmed. I’ll meet you there.

Also — let’s plan to talk through the budget tonight after the kids are down. James can walk us through his spreadsheet, and I’ll pull the updated preapproval letter from Dan so we have a hard ceiling walking in.

No pressure either way. Just want us on the same page before we write an offer.

Megan
